definition: Catalog of the most frequent ICT methodology errors and their fixes. Each mistake maps to the specific concept(s) violated and provides the corrective action.
feeds_into: entry_models (gates: each mistake maps to a grade reduction or skip)
bridge_integration: Informational meta-card. The six listed mistakes are NOT loaded directly by any module — bridge/claude_decision.py never calls _load_json on common_mistakes.json, and bridge/concept_injector.py:225-237 + _select_relevant_concepts() do not include 'common_mistakes' in their pick lists. The card lives in _index.json under 'sections' (line 331) but the dependency-traversal walker in concept_injector.py:_get_relevant_concepts (line 89-155) only walks 'concept_dependency_graph' / per-layer 'concepts' arrays, not 'sections'. Each individual mistake is, however, enforced ELSEWHERE by code that doesn't reference this meta-card: (1) mistake_1_chasing (entering during manipulation) — synergy_scorer.py 'Kill zone gates session score during accumulation' anti-synergy (line 488-492, -3); claude_decision.py:295-297 active_conflict 'Displacement in accumulation phase'. (2) mistake_2_wrong_zone (BUY in premium / SELL in discount) — claude_decision.py:575-585 ZONE GATE (hard SKIP); HTF zone check at lines 587-611 (Grade A→B cap). (3) mistake_3_ignoring_htf — claude_decision.py HTF Alignment Gate (pre-gate #5 per ARCHITECTURE.md § 4.1, SKIP if non-scalp opposes H4 bias); HTF Data Gate (#4, caps Grade A to B if W1/D1/H4 missing); live_executor_adapter.py:_check_htf_invalidation (post-entry close on D1+H4 both opposing). (4) mistake_4_tight_stops (SL at exact zone edge) — claude_decision.py SL validators enforce 2.5x ATR floor for crypto / 2x ATR for others (lines 818-820); risk_bridge.py minimum SL distance check. (5) mistake_5_no_DOL — claude_decision.py:657-684 hard DOL pre-filter SKIP at <4x ATR (Grade A/B exempt). (6) mistake_6_no_displacement — synergy_scorer.py:482-486 'Displacement gates OB validity' gate (-4); ict_pipeline.py:509-512 detect_order_blocks(require_fvg=True) at detection time so unvalidated OBs never make it into ob_score; claude_decision.py:299-301 active_conflict 'OB without displacement = unproven zone'. The mapping above is the integration: this card is essentially a human-readable index of code-enforced rules, NOT itself executed. If a future code path wants to use it programmatically, the obvious extension is to load it in concept_injector.py and surface 1-2 relevant mistakes per signal as prompt warnings — but that work has not been done. (Note: the section _purpose plus its 'concepts_violated' / 'fix' structure makes this card useful as a Claude reference document when she reads the KB directly via grep/read tools, separate from the prompt-injection pipeline.)

mistake_1_chasing.what: Entering during manipulation instead of waiting for distribution retracement
mistake_1_chasing.fix: Wait for displacement AFTER sweep. Enter the retracement.

mistake_2_wrong_zone.what: Buying in premium or selling in discount

mistake_3_ignoring_htf.fix: HTF = direction. LTF = timing. Never trade LTF against HTF.

concepts_violated: liquidity (your SL IS liquidity that will be swept)
mistake_4_tight_stops.fix: Buffer beyond manipulation zone. Minimum 1.5x ATR.

mistake_5_no_DOL.fix: No DOL = no clear TP = skip.

mistake_6_no_displacement.what: Entering at OB/FVG that was NOT preceded by displacement
mistake_6_no_displacement.fix: OB/FVG without displacement = unproven zone. Wait for displacement to validate.
